{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer Jo",
  "description": "Bell OIl and Gas Limited is a leadingindigenous oilfield services company, dedicated to consistently proffering solutions, driving local content development and capacity building in Nigeria.

We offer an extensive range of high quality professional services to clients across the facets of oil and gas industry and other industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Onsite)
Reports to: Head of HR/Assistant Manager-HR

Responsibilities


	Right application of laws governing HR Practice in Nigeria
	Ensuring effective new staff induction programmes.
	Effective HMO Liaison and management.
	Effective PFAs/ Group Life Insurance company liaison and management.
	Leave process management.
	Approved travel entitlements management.
	Approved training entitlements management.
	Management of staff notable dates and events.
	Drawing up and managing staff motivation programmes.
	Effective management of staff personal data.
	Effective staff exit management.
	Driving company-wide staff engagement programmes
	Leadership and people management.


Required Competencies / Person Specification


	A good University degree
	HR Certification (CIPM/SHRM/HRCI), an added advantage.
	Minimum of 3 years cognate experience
	Proven experience in a well-structured corporate environment
	Strong working knowledge of Nigerian HR laws and statutory compliance
	High level of confidentiality, professionalism, and integrity
	Advanced proficiency in HRIS, Excel, and HR reporting
	Strong stakeholder management and communication capability
	Strong interpersonal skill
	Strong employee relations and case management skills
	HR operations and compliance management
	Stakeholder engagement and advisory capability
	Report writing and HR analytics
	Sound MS Word and Excel skills
	Oral &amp; written communication
	Very structured work environment experience.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV as an attachment&nbsp;to:&nbsp;manpower@belloil.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Human Resources Officer Jo at Bell OIl and Gas Limited




        
    

        
        Bell OIl and Gas Limited is a leadingindigenous oilfield services company, dedicated to consistently proffering solutions, driving local content development and capacity building in Nigeria.

We offer an extensive range of high quality professional services to clients across the facets of oil and gas industry and other industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Onsite)
Reports to: Head of HR/Assistant Manager-HR

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Right application of laws governing HR Practice in Nigeria
	Ensuring effective new staff induction programmes.
	Effective HMO Liaison and management.
	Effective PFAs/ Group Life Insurance company liaison and management.
	Leave process management.
	Approved travel entitlements management.
	Approved training entitlements management.
	Management of staff notable dates and events.
	Drawing up and managing staff motivation programmes.
	Effective management of staff personal data.
	Effective staff exit management.
	Driving company-wide staff engagement programmes
	Leadership and people management.


Required Competencies / Person Specification


	A good University degree
	HR Certification (CIPM/SHRM/HRCI), an added advantage.
	Minimum of 3 years cognate experience
	Proven experience in a well-structured corporate environment
	Strong working knowledge of Nigerian HR laws and statutory compliance
	High level of confidentiality, professionalism, and integrity
	Advanced proficiency in HRIS, Excel, and HR reporting
	Strong stakeholder management and communication capability
	Strong interpersonal skill
	Strong employee relations and case management skills
	HR operations and compliance management
	Stakeholder engagement and advisory capability
	Report writing and HR analytics
	Sound MS Word and Excel skills
	Oral & written communication
	Very structured work environment experience.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV as an attachment to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

title: Female Front Desk Officer Job at Pyfinest Resources
content:
Pyfinest Resources - Our client, a logistics powerhouse, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Female Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Welcome clients, visitors, and partners with professionalism and warmth
	Manage incoming calls, emails, and customer inquiries efficiently
	Provide accurate information about shipments, tracking, and company services
	Coordinate courier bookings and support walk-in customers
	Maintain visitor logs and ensure compliance with security procedures
	Manage front desk documentation and basic administrative tasks
	Support operations team with scheduling and internal coordination
	Ensure the reception area reflects the company’s professional brand image


Ideal Candidate Profile


	Minimum of OND /HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration or related field
	1–3 years of experience in front desk, customer service, or administrative role
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Professional appearance and confident demeanor
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Ability to multitask and stay organized in a fast-paced logistics environment
	High level of responsibility, discretion, and attention to detail.


Why This Role is Attractive


	Salary: N100,000 monthly
	Be the face of a reputable logistics brand
	Work in a structured, growth-focused environment
	Career growth opportunities within the organization
	Exposure to courier and cargo logistics operations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Operations & Logistics Officer – Port Harcourt” as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only candidates with verifiable logistics leadership experience will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Control Manager",
  "description": "CareerHive Management -&nbsp;We pride ourselves in bringing the world of work together through technology by recruiting highly qualified technical &amp; non technical talents for your brand.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Internal Control Manager to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will have practical experience in internal control processes, with the ability to identify risks, support control measures, and ensure operational compliance.
	You should have intermediate analytical skills, excellent time management, and reporting abilities.
	Retail industry experience is an added advantage.


Key Responsibilities


	Implementation and continuous improvement of internal control policies and procedures across business operations.
	Conduct regular internal control checks, audits, and reviews to ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Identify areas of process improvement and recommend corrective actions to strengthen internal control systems.
	Monitor daily operational activities and ensure proper documentation and adherence to internal control standards.
	Prepare periodic reports and presentations on internal control findings for management review.
	Perform data analysis using Excel to identify trends, discrepancies, and potential risks.
	Support investigations into operational breaches, discrepancies, or fraudulent activities.
	Collaborate with various departments to ensure smooth implementation of internal control measures.
	Participate in risk assessments and assist in the design of control measures for new processes.
	Track corrective action plans and ensure timely closure of internal control gaps.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications and work experience.
	3 years &amp; Above of relevant work experience in internal control, audit, or compliance roles.
	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Intermediate analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and identify risk patterns.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel.
	Excellent time management skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good reporting and documentation skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
	Retail industry experience is an added advantage.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Odoo) is a plus.
	Familiarity with audit or control tools is an added benefit.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 / month (Based on Experience).",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

Internal Control Manager Job at CareerHive Management




        
    

        
        CareerHive Management - We pride ourselves in bringing the world of work together through technology by recruiting highly qualified technical & non technical talents for your brand.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Internal Control Manager to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will have practical experience in internal control processes, with the ability to identify risks, support control measures, and ensure operational compliance.
	You should have intermediate analytical skills, excellent time management, and reporting abilities.
	Retail industry experience is an added advantage.


Key Responsibilities


	Implementation and continuous improvement of internal control policies and procedures across business operations.
	Conduct regular internal control checks, audits, and reviews to ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Identify areas of process improvement and recommend corrective actions to strengthen internal control systems.
	Monitor daily operational activities and ensure proper documentation and adherence to internal control standards.
	Prepare periodic reports and presentations on internal control findings for management review.
	Perform data analysis using Excel to identify trends, discrepancies, and potential risks.
	Support investigations into operational breaches, discrepancies, or fraudulent activities.
	Collaborate with various departments to ensure smooth implementation of internal control measures.
	Participate in risk assessments and assist in the design of control measures for new processes.
	Track corrective action plans and ensure timely closure of internal control gaps.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications and work experience.
	3 years & Above of relevant work experience in internal control, audit, or compliance roles.
	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Intermediate analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and identify risk patterns.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel.
	Excellent time management skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good reporting and documentation skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
	Retail industry experience is an added advantage.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Odoo) is a plus.
	Familiarity with audit or control tools is an added benefit.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 / month (Based on Experience).    

    

        Click here to Apply

Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications and work experience.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;

IT Officer Job at Casa 45 Enterprises Limited




        
    

        
        Casa 45 Enterprises Limited is an Afro luxury lounge and Restaurant situated at 35, Adeola Odeku Street, Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Officer

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The IT Officer manages and maintains the organization's technology infrastructure, ensuring seamless operation of hardware, software, and networks.
	Provide user support, implementing cybersecurity measures, managing data, and troubleshooting technical issues to support daily operations and special events.


Key Responsibilities and Duties


	Network and System Administration: Configuring, maintaining, and upgrading the club’s computer networks and servers to ensure uptime.
	Technical Support: Providing helpdesk support for members, staff, and guests, resolving hardware and software issues.
	Security and Data Management: Implementing antivirus, firewall protection, and maintaining data security and access controls.
	Infrastructure Maintenance: Installing, configuring, and updating hardware (computers, POS systems, printers) and software applications.
	User Account Management: Handling the creation, maintenance, and deletion of user accounts for new, existing, or departing staff/members.
	Event Technology Support: Ensuring audio-visual equipment, Wi-Fi, and other technical requirements are functional for events.
	Vendor Management: Coordinating with external IT service providers or vendors for repairs and maintenance when necessary.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications and work experience.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior HRBP / Performance, Reward & HR Services Manager",
  "description": "Bourbon Interoil Nigeria Limited, as leading international player in marine services, offers to the most demanding oil &amp; gas clients worldwide a full line of innovative, safe, high performance and new generation vessels and an expanded offer of offshore oil &amp; gas services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:&nbsp;

Job Position:&nbsp;Senior HRBP / Performance, Reward &amp; HR Services Manager

Reference ID:&nbsp;2026-2147
Job Location:&nbsp;Amadi Base, Rivers
Contract type:&nbsp;Permanent Contract

Role Summary


	We are seeking a seasoned HR leader to provide&nbsp;senior HR Business Partnering&nbsp;to a defined client population while owning&nbsp;company-wide responsibility for HR policy, performance management, HR shared services, and total reward.
	The role operates at the heart of the business, balancing strategic advisory input with strong governance, cost discipline, and operational execution in a safety-critical offshore logistics environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Act as a trusted HR adviser to senior leaders on people strategy, organization design, performance, and workforce planning.
	Own and govern all HR policies, ensuring consistency and compliance with Nigerian labor law.
	Lead the design and operation of the performance management framework, including goal setting and performance&ndash;reward linkage.
	Oversee HR shared services, including payroll, HR administration, data integrity, and service standards.
	Lead total reward strategy covering salary structures, incentives, benefits, and annual reward cycles.
	Provide workforce cost, performance, and reward insights to senior management.


Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in HR, Business, Economics, or a related discipline; Master&rsquo;s degree or professional HR certification (CIPM, SHRM-SCP, CIPD) preferred.


Experience


	12&ndash;15 years of progressive HR experience, including senior HR Business Partnering.
	Proven experience leading HR policy, performance management, reward, and HR operations/shared services.
	Experience in offshore logistics, marine services, oil &amp; gas support, or a similarly complex, regulated environment is an advantage.
	Strong commercial acumen, governance mindset, and senior stakeholder influencing skills.
",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Senior HRBP / Performance, Reward & HR Services Manager Job at Bourbon Interoil Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Bourbon Interoil Nigeria Limited, as leading international player in marine services, offers to the most demanding oil & gas clients worldwide a full line of innovative, safe, high performance and new generation vessels and an expanded offer of offshore oil & gas services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below: 

Job Position: Senior HRBP / Performance, Reward & HR Services Manager

Reference ID: 2026-2147
Job Location: Amadi Base, Rivers
Contract type: Permanent Contract

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a seasoned HR leader to provide senior HR Business Partnering to a defined client population while owning company-wide responsibility for HR policy, performance management, HR shared services, and total reward.
	The role operates at the heart of the business, balancing strategic advisory input with strong governance, cost discipline, and operational execution in a safety-critical offshore logistics environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Act as a trusted HR adviser to senior leaders on people strategy, organization design, performance, and workforce planning.
	Own and govern all HR policies, ensuring consistency and compliance with Nigerian labor law.
	Lead the design and operation of the performance management framework, including goal setting and performance–reward linkage.
	Oversee HR shared services, including payroll, HR administration, data integrity, and service standards.
	Lead total reward strategy covering salary structures, incentives, benefits, and annual reward cycles.
	Provide workforce cost, performance, and reward insights to senior management.


Requirements
Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s Degree in HR, Business, Economics, or a related discipline; Master’s degree or professional HR certification (CIPM, SHRM-SCP, CIPD) preferred.


Experience


	12–15 years of progressive HR experience, including senior HR Business Partnering.
	Proven experience leading HR policy, performance management, reward, and HR operations/shared services.
	Experience in offshore logistics, marine services, oil & gas support, or a similarly complex, regulated environment is an advantage.
	Strong commercial acumen, governance mindset, and senior stakeholder influencing skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
